converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Henry Salem Hubbell papers
Hubbell, Henry Salem, 1869-1949.
1955
5 cubic feet. 5 cubic feet.
1800-1975. Correspondence, research and lecture notes, catalogs, newspaper clippings, scripts, theatrical programs, photographs, audiotapes, manuscripts, and other papers. Correspondence and other papers of Henry Salem Hubbell, a portrait artist and educator. Collection material in English 2081797
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
M.E. Warren
Date completed:
August 1991
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, March 2003
Date modified:
Fredrika Loew, October 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Henry Salem Hubbell, a portrait artist, was the head of the Carnegie Institute of Technology School of Painting and Decoration in Pittsburgh, 1918-1921.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Letters (1897-1907) from Hubbell, when an art student in Paris, Etaples, and Madrid, and from his wife, (Nellie) Rose Strong Hubbell, largely to his benefactor Mrs. Lydis Avery Coonley Ward; correspondence (1891-1944) between Hubbell and his family; research and lecture notes; correspondence, catalogs, and newspaper clippings concerning his paintings and exhibitions; scripts, programs, and notices of amateur plays in which he was either an actor or set designer; and photographs of himself and his paintings, studios, homes, and family. Also, tape recordings, notes, and correspondence of his son, Henry Willard Hubbell, concerning his father's life and work; typescripts and proofs of the journals, plays and short stories, articles, and verse written by Mrs. Hubbell; photocopies and typescripts of papers dealing with the history and genealogy of the Hubbell (Hubbels, Hubbel), Gleason, Rockwell, and related families (1800-1940); and proposals concerning the role of art education at the University of Miami.
Cite As:
Henry Salem Hubbell papers, #1955. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Hubbell, Nellie Rose Strong. Sketches. Scrapbooks. Poems. Photographs. Lecture notes. Genealogies. Painting. Literature. Education in art. Art. Amateur theater. Hubbell, Henry Willard. Hubbell, Rose Strong. Ward, Lydis Avery Coonley. Hubbell family. Hubbels family. Hubbell family. Gleason family. Rockwell family. Carnegie Institute of Technology. School of Painting and Decoration
